name: dynamic-chunking-diffusion
description: Apply Dynamic Chunking Diffusion Transformer to dynamic problems. Technique: Diffusion Transformers process images as fixed-length sequences of tokens produc...
content:
## Overview

This skill implements the technique from "Dynamic Chunking Diffusion Transformer".

Key contribution: Diffusion Transformers process images as fixed-length sequences of tokens produced by a static $\textit{patchify}$ operation. While effective, this design spends uniform compute on low- and high-information regions alike, ignoring that images contain regions of varying detail and that the denoising process progresses from coarse structure at early timesteps to fine detail at late timesteps.

## When to use

- Apply when solving dynamic problems
- When baseline approaches are insufficient
- Working with structured problem spaces

## When NOT to use

- If problem constraints limit complexity
- When simpler methods achieve target performance
- Time-critical scenarios with strict latency needs

## Core Technique

The paper proposes an approach through:

1. Problem formulation from the paper
2. Algorithm implementation as described
3. Integration with existing systems
4. Evaluation against baselines

## Implementation

1. Understand problem requirements
2. Implement core algorithm components
3. Configure evaluation metrics from paper
4. Compare against baseline methods
5. Tune hyperparameters for your domain

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.06351
